docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
This section will guide you through the complete setup of University Curriculum Enabling Tool.

  1. Quick Start – Install dependencies and run the tool.
  2. User Guides – Learn how to use the tool for your role.

docs.title: Need Help?
docs.text:
If you encounter issues during setup:

  1. Check the Troubleshooting guide
  2. Visit GitHub* Issues for community support

docs.title: University Curriculum Enabling Tool
docs.text:
University Curriculum Enabling Tool is an advanced application leveraging fine-tuned Language Models and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) technology to support diverse academic needs:

  • Curriculum Builder: Designed for faculty, this tool streamlines the creation and refinement of course content, enabling the development of tailored curricula and continuous improvement of teaching materials.
  • Expert Advisor: Lecturers can customize and enhance their teaching resources, generate assignments, quizzes, and research prompts, and access tools to support effective course delivery.
  • Learning Companion: Students benefit from personalized learning experiences, including practice quizzes, material summarization, and interactive note-taking, both within and beyond the content provided by lecturers.

Powered by Intel CPUs and GPUs, the tool ensures accurate, contextually relevant responses, making it a valuable asset for curriculum development, teaching, and student learning.

docs.title: Software Requirements
docs.text:
• Operating System:
Type Version Linux Ubuntu 24.04 Desktop LTS Windows Windows 11

docs.title: Hardware Requirements
docs.text:
• CPU: Intel® Core™ Ultra Processor (Products formerly Meteor Lake-UH) and above
  • RAM: Minimum: 32GB, Recommended: 64GB
  • Storage: 256GB free space
  • GPU: At least one of the following:
Type Model Recommended For Integrated Intel® Arc™ Graphics Basic usage Discrete Intel® Arc™ A770 Graphics Enhanced performance

docs.title: Local Ports Usage
docs.text:
These ports are only used on the local machine (localhost/127.0.0.1) and don't need to be exposed externally.

Port Service Required For 8080 Web Interface Application UI access 8016 Backend Internal REST API 5950 Ollama Model Serving LLM inference and embeddings

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text: Important Notice: This software is currently in pre-production status, designed to run locally on a single system only. For more stable version, please refer to our latest tagged pre-release.

docs.title: Quick Start Linux
docs.text:
Pre-requisite: Follow the Edge Developer Kit Reference Scripts to install the necessary drivers and compute-related packages for Intel® GPUs

  1. Setup - Install system dependencies:

Note: Setup requires administrator privileges as it installs system-level dependencies.

sudo ./setup.sh
  1. Install - Install the application from sources to build and package the application. From here, running below will prompt you to enter select either ollama or ovms: 
    ./install.sh
    However, you can also directly select an AI provider to install via command:
  2. OpenVINO Model Server (OVMS) 
    PROVIDER=ovms ./install.sh

  3. Ollama 

    PROVIDER=ollama ./install.sh

  4. Continue Installation - Go to the generated distribution package directory (follow the instruction from previous command):

  5. Run - Start the application: 

    ./run.sh

  6. Access - Open the web interface at http://localhost:8080

  7. Stop - To stop the application, run: 

    ./stop.sh

  8. Uninstall - To remove the application, run: 

    ./uninstall.sh

docs.title: Quick Start Windows
docs.text:
1. System-level setup (Adminstrator required)

# Double click on setup_win.bat and select "Yes"
.\setup_win.bat
This script will perform or install the following if not present and may take a while to complete: - Winget - Python 3.12 (if not not installed or lower version) - Enable PowerShell script execution if needed

  1. Install application and its dependencies

# Double click on install_win.bat
.\install_win.bat
This will automatically proceed to installation of application (without administrator privilege) which does the following: - Download and install Node.js locally (22.16.0) - Download and install jq locally - Install npm dependencies - Set up Python virtual environment - Download and configure Ollama - Create environment configuration files

  1. Start the application 

    # Double-click to run
.\run_win.bat
    Running this command will automatically open a web-browser with http://localhost:8080

  2. Stop the application 

    # Double click to stop all services
.\stop_win.bat

    IMPORTANT: Please make sure to close all command or terminal prompts that are open after running stop_win.bat

  3. Uninstall the application 

    # Double click to run uninstall script
.\uninstall_win.bat

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
The application is now running. Select your persona to get started:

Persona Intended For Guide Curriculum Builder Faculty View guide Expert Advisor Lecturers View guide Learning Companion Students View guide

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
1. Startup application on Windows

On Windows, when running run_win.bat, PM2 launches several command prompt windows during operation. These windows can be minimized, but they will remain open. To stop the application, run stop_win.bat and manually close the command prompt windows to properly shut down all services.

  1. Running OVMS on Windows

When running any workloads in Windows (e.g. Summary generation), additional command prompts may pop-up repeatingly until the workload has successfully completed.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
1. Unable to unzip file from Github for Windows

If you have trouble unzipping the downloaded zip file from GitHub on Windows, try extracting it to a folder with a shorter name or path. This issue is caused by Windows' maximum file path length limitation.

docs.title: Disclaimer
docs.text: Intel is committed to respecting human rights and avoiding causing or contributing to adverse impacts on human rights. See Intel’s Global Human Rights Principles. Intel’s products and software are intended only to be used in applications that do not cause or contribute to adverse impacts on human rights. Users should comply with all requirements to notify relevant parties that AI was used in the production of materials, as mandated by their employers or professional standards.

docs.title: User Guides
docs.text: University Curriculum Enabling Tool supports three distinct personas, each tailored to a specific academic role. Select your persona below to get started.

docs.title: Curriculum Builder
docs.text:
Designed for faculty members responsible for institutional curriculum design.

Key capabilities:

  • Add and manage AI language models
  • Create and manage courses and programmes
  • Export an installation package for Expert Advisors and Learning Companions

Curriculum Builder Guide →

docs.title: Expert Advisor
docs.text:
Designed for lecturers who deliver courses using a pre-configured installation package.

Key capabilities:

  • Load courses from an installation package
  • Create presentation slides, FAQs, assessments, and quizzes
  • Chat with an AI model, optionally using RAG documents as context

Expert Advisor Guide →

docs.title: Learning Companion
docs.text:
Designed for students engaging with course content and personal study materials.

Key capabilities:

  • Access available courses
  • Generate summaries, FAQs, and quizzes from uploaded documents
  • Chat with an AI model, optionally using RAG documents as context

Learning Companion Guide →

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text:
This guide covers common issues you may encounter when using University Curriculum Enabling Tool.

Documentation in progress.

docs.title: Curriculum Builder
docs.text:
The Curriculum Builder is designed for faculty members to create and refine course content, build structured curricula, and continuously improve teaching materials.

This guide walks through the full workflow: Landing page → Model → Courses → Programme → Download Installation Package.

docs.title: Select Curriculum Builder Persona
docs.text:
1. On the application home page, click Curriculum Builder Persona.

  2. Click Get Started.

docs.title: Landing Page
docs.text: After selecting the persona, the landing page displays a summary of existing models, courses, and programmes.

docs.title: Model
docs.text:
At least one AI model must be added before creating a course. The model provides the intelligence behind course content generation.

  1. Click Add Model on the Model page.

  2. Select a model from the Ollama* Library and click Download.

    Info

    The available model sources depend on the AI Model Provider configured during setup.

  3. Once the download completes, the model appears on the Model page and is ready to use.

docs.title: Courses
docs.text:
A course is the core unit of content in the curriculum. Each course is linked to an AI model that powers its learning experience. Create at least one course before building a programme.

  1. Click Create Course on the Course page.

  2. Fill in the required course details and click Next.

  3. Select the downloaded model in the Model section, then click Create Course.

  4. The Course page reloads displaying the newly created course.

docs.title: Programme
docs.text:
A programme groups one or more courses into a structured learning path. Create a programme and assign courses to it before exporting the installation package for the Expert Advisor and Learning Companion.

  1. Click Create Programme on the Programme page.

  2. Fill in the required programme details and click Next.

  3. Select the course in the Available Courses section, then click Create Programme.

  4. The Programme page reloads displaying the newly created programme.

docs.title: Download Installation Package
docs.text:
The installation package bundles the programme content for distribution to Expert Advisor and Learning Companion users.

  1. On the Programmes dashboard, click ... beside the target programme and select Download Installation Package.

  2. Select the target user package and click Download Package. The package downloads automatically and is ready to share with the target users.

docs.title: AI Chat
docs.text:
The Chat page allows you to chat with a downloaded model using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).

  1. Select the model from the top-left corner.

  2. Click Add Source in the bottom-left corner.

  3. Upload your document and click Upload.

    Info

    The currently supported format is PDF only.

  4. Check the checkbox next to a source in the sources list.

    Info

    Selecting a document as a knowledge source is optional.

  5. Start chatting with the selected AI model.

docs.title: Expert Advisor
docs.text:
The Expert Advisor is designed for lecturers who deliver courses using a pre-configured installation package provided by a Curriculum Builder.

This guide covers the full workflow: Landing Page → Add Courses → Add RAG Document Source → Course Content Generator → Summary Generator → Assessment Generator → FAQ Generator → AI Chat.

docs.title: Select Expert Advisor Persona
docs.text:
1. On the application home page, click Expert Advisor Persona.

  2. Click Get Started.

docs.title: Landing Page
docs.text: After selecting Get Started as Expert Advisor, the landing page is displayed.

docs.title: Add Courses
docs.text:
1. Click Courses then click Add Courses to upload courses from the installation package.

  2. Upload the programme configuration file.

    The programme configuration JSON file is included in the extracted installation package.

    Info

    Example programme configuration file name format:

    programme-<programme_code>-<programme_version>-UCET-<application_version>.json

    After uploading the programme configuration file, click Next.

  3. Select the courses to add.

    Choose the target courses from the available courses list, then click Add Selected Courses.

  4. Select the active course.

    Select a course from the course selector in the top-left corner.

docs.title: Add RAG Document Source
docs.text:
Uploaded sources are used for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enhance AI responses across all features.

  1. Click Add Source in the bottom-left corner.

  2. Upload your document and click Upload.

    Info

    The currently supported format is PDF only.

  3. Check the checkbox next to a source in the sources list.

    Info

    Selecting a document as a knowledge source is optional.

docs.title: Course Content Generator
docs.text:
Generate teaching materials with RAG-powered content based on your selected source documents.

Info

The quality of generated content depends on the relevance and quality of your source materials.

  1. Click Slide, then click Create Teaching Materials.

  2. Enter the topic name, configure the available options, then click Generate Teaching Materials.

  3. Once generated, use the tabs to navigate between sections. To download, click the PDF or PowerPoint* icon at the bottom of the page.

docs.title: Summary Generator
docs.text:
Generate a summary from a single uploaded document.

Info

Only one document can be selected at a time for summary generation.

  1. Select a document using the file selector in the sidebar.

  2. Select the active course from the course selector in the top-left corner.

  3. Click Generate Summary at the bottom of the page.

  4. Once the summary is generated, scroll to the bottom to find the export options. Select your preferred format to download.

docs.title: Assessment Generator
docs.text:
1. Click the Assessment tab, then select the assessment type. The steps below use Quiz as an example.

  2. Click Create Quiz.

  3. Enter the quiz title, configure the quiz type using the available options, then click Generate Quiz.

  4. Review the generated quiz, then click Save Draft or Publish Quiz.

  5. The generated quiz appears in Your Quiz History.

docs.title: FAQ Generator
docs.text:
1. Check the checkbox next to a source in the sources list to apply a document source.

  2. Configure the FAQ Settings, then click Generate FAQs.

    Info

    Optionally enter keywords to focus on specific topics.

  3. Review the generated FAQs. Click Continue to generate more FAQs based on the source document.

docs.title: Learning Companion
docs.text:
The Learning Companion is designed for students who want to independently explore course materials, test their understanding, and plan their studies using AI-powered tools.

This guide covers the full workflow: Landing Page → Add Courses → Add RAG Document Source → Summary Generator → Quiz Generator → FAQ Generator → AI Chat → Personalized Study Plan Generator.

docs.title: Select Learning Companion Persona
docs.text:
1. On the application home page, click Learning Companion Persona.

  2. Click Get Started.

docs.title: Landing Page
docs.text: After selecting Get Started as Learning Companion, the landing page is displayed. Click Add Courses to upload courses from the installation package.

docs.title: Add Courses
docs.text:
1. Click Courses then click Add Courses to upload courses from the installation package.

  2. Upload the programme configuration file.

    The programme configuration JSON file is included in the extracted installation package.

    Info

    Example programme configuration file name format:

    programme-<programme_code>-<programme_version>-UCET-<application_version>.json

    After uploading the programme configuration file, click Next.

  3. Select the courses to add.

    Choose the target courses from the available courses list, then click Add Selected Courses.

  4. Select the active course.

    Select a course from the course selector in the top-left corner.

docs.title: Add RAG Document Source
docs.text:
Uploaded sources are used for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enhance AI responses across all features.

  1. Click Add Source in the bottom-left corner.

  2. Upload your document and click Upload.

    Info

    The currently supported format is PDF only.

  3. Check the checkbox next to a source in the sources list.

    Info

    Selecting a document as a knowledge source is optional.

docs.title: Summary Generator
docs.text:
Generate a summary from a single uploaded document.

Info

Only one document can be selected at a time for summary generation.

  1. Select a document using the file selector in the sidebar.

  2. Select the active course from the course selector in the top-left corner.

  3. Click Generate Summary at the bottom of the page.

  4. Once the summary is generated, scroll to the bottom to find the export options. Select your preferred format to download.

docs.title: Quiz Generator
docs.text:
The Quiz Generator lets you create quizzes based on your learning materials.

  1. In the Quiz tab, click Create Quiz.

  2. Configure the quiz type using the available options, then click Start Now!.

  3. Answer the quiz questions and click Next.

  4. Review the quiz summary.

docs.title: FAQ Generator
docs.text:
1. Check the checkbox next to a source in the sources list to apply a document source.

  2. Configure the FAQ Settings, then click Generate FAQs.

    Info

    Optionally enter keywords to focus on specific topics.

  3. Review the generated FAQs. Click Continue to generate more FAQs based on the source document.

docs.title: Personalized Study Plan Generator
docs.text:
1. Upload or select your learning materials using the source selector in the sidebar, then click Create Study Plan.

  2. Configure your study period, available time, and difficulty level, then click Generate Study Plan.

  3. View your study plan with weekly schedules and resources.

    Info

    Download your study plan as a PDF for offline reference.
